name: NeuroKit2 (Physiological Signal Processing)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Dominique Makowski / Neuropsychology Lab

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; fast for typical ECG/EEG/EDA signal lengths

agent_readiness.notes: Physiological signal processing for ECG, EEG, EMG, EDA, PPG. import neurokit2 as nk. ecg_cleaned = nk.ecg_clean(ecg_signal, sampling_rate=1000). signals, info = nk.ecg_process(ecg_signal, sampling_rate=1000). nk.ecg_plot(signals, info). HRV: hrv = nk.hrv(peaks, sampling_rate=1000). EDA: eda_cleaned = nk.eda_clean(eda_signal). nk.eda_process(). EEG: nk.eeg_badchannels(). RSP: nk.rsp_process(). PPG: nk.ppg_process(). Simulation: nk.ecg_simulate(duration=10, sampling_rate=1000). Complexity: nk.complexity_entropy(). Event segmentation. Epoch extraction.

gotchas: Not a real-time processing library — batch processing only
ECG peak detection algorithm choice matters (neurokit2 has several)
EEG processing is basic — use MNE-Python for full EEG pipeline
Signal quality assessment methods can give false confidence
best_when: ECG/HRV analysis: R-peak detection, heart rate variability metrics
Multi-signal physiological analysis (ECG + EDA + RSP together)
summary: NeuroKit2 processes physiological signals: ECG, EEG, EDA, PPG, RSP. ecg_process(signal, sr=1000). hrv(peaks). eda_clean(). complexity_entropy(). ecg_simulate() for testing. Multi-signal epoch extraction. MIT by Dominique Makowski. Best for HRV and EDA research. Batch only, no streaming. EEG is basic — use MNE for full pipeline. Multiple peak detection algorithms available. No type annotations.
